name: Spanish Seafood Filling (for Pie Crust-Style
recipeInstructions:
-------------
Filling:
Olive oil - 4 tbsp
Onion, finely chopped - 2
Garlic, minced - 2 cloves
Green bell pepper, finely chopped - 1
Red pepper, finely chopped - 1
Bay leaf - 1
Tomatoes, skinned and seeded - 2 (chopped)
Tiny bay scallops - 1/2 lb
Medium shrimp, shelled - 1/2 lb
Anchovy, minced - 2 tbsp
Parsley, minced - 2 tbsp
Lemon zest, finely minced - 1/4 tsp
Salt and freshly ground pepper
Combine olive oil, onion, garlic, green and red peppers, and bay leaf in a pan. Cook until peppers are very soft. Add tomatoes and cook until slightly thickened. Add scallops and shrimp; cook for 2 to 3 minutes or until opaque. Stir in anchovy, parsley, and lemon zest. Season with salt and pepper.
Pastry:
Combine flour and salt. Cut lard (or shortening) into the mixture until it resembles coarse crumbs. Mix water and lemon juice (or vinegar) with egg yolks. Add to flour mixture; knead into a ball. Chill for 30 minutes. Roll out into a large, thin rectangle measuring approximately 16 x 20 inches. Cut into squares of about 4 x 4 inches. Chill until filling is ready.
Assembly:
Place 2 to 3 tablespoons of the seafood filling in each pastry square. Fold over and seal the edges. Trim excess pastry while maintaining the triangle shape. Brush egg wash (egg beaten with milk, cream, or water) onto turnovers. Place on a greased baking sheet. Bake in a preheated oven at 375°F until golden, about 15 minutes. Serve warm or at room temperature. Makes approximately 4 pastries.
